name: Resource block
description: The resource block is a content unit that represents an individual resource item within a listing. It displays a title, a cover image, a description, and citation metadata. The block supports a range of resource types, including blog posts, videos, webinars, case studies, solution briefs, white papers, and reports. This block is used as the primary content unit in the [Blog](/sites/construct/blog) and [Resources](/sites/construct/resources).
usage:
Use Resource block to present scannable listings of editorial or knowledge-driven content. It supports a range of resource types, including blog posts, videos, webinars, case studies, solution briefs, white papers, and reports.

### Where to use

Resource block can appear in the following patterns and contexts:

*   [Resources](/sites/construct/resources)
*   [Blog](/sites/construct/blog)
*   [Tab section](/sites/construct/tab-section)

### Layout

Resource block comes in two layouts:

**[Horizontal layout](/sites/construct/resource-block#anatomy)** : It places content in a single row, with the image on one side and the title, description, and citation stacked in a column on the other. The cover image is optional. When included, it can be either a 16:9 image or a fixed-size logo.

**[Vertical layout](/sites/construct/resource-block#anatomy)** **:** It stacks content in a single column in the order: image, title, description, and citation. The cover image is required and uses a 16:9 ratio.

### When to use

*   Each item represents a standalone piece of content with a title and short preview
*   Users need to scan multiple items quickly before navigating to one
*   Content may be dynamically fetched or statically configured

### When not to use

*   When the content is purely textual without article-level separation, use Tiered block or [Equal height block](/sites/construct/equal-height-block) instead
*   If items require rich structured elements (such as lists, code blocks, or calls to action) within the preview, consider converting them into a section-level layout using a [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) or [Divided section](/sites/construct/divided-section) instead

table.name: Horizontal layout
table.description: The layout divided into two columns that presents elements horizontally.

table.name: Vertical layout
table.description: The layout that presents elements within a single column.

table.name: Cover image
table.description: 16:9 cover image or logo(horizontal layout only) representing the resource. In dynamic content, it displays the content’s cover image. In static content, a custom image can be provided.

table.name: Heading
table.description: The resource heading displays the full title of the resource and functions as a link.

table.name: Body
table.description: In dynamic content, the body displays the first 180 characters of the content. In static content, it serves as a custom introductory context for the content.

table.name: Citation metadata
table.description: The citation is an optional author/date field in small text and supports showing either or both.

properties.name: Article title
properties.description: Title configuration for the article; functions as a link to the resource

properties.name: Article description
properties.description:
Description text for the article
Dynamic: displays the first 180 characters of the article. 
Static: a custom description should be provided.
properties.constraint:
Dynamic: 180 characters (with the truncation)
Static: up to 300 characters

properties.name: Citation author
properties.description: Author name text in the citation

properties.name: Citation date
properties.description: Publishing date in the citation format “DD Month YYYY”

properties.name: Image
properties.description: Cover image for the article. In the blog pattern, it is required and falls back to a default image when not provided. In the resources pattern, it supports 16:9 image, logo, or none as options.

properties.name: Citation
properties.description: Author/date field in small text and supports showing either or both.
